name: Pasta with Pesto, Trapani-Style
intro:
I first tried this pesto in Trapani, western Sicily. The almonds were still green outside so the kernels were soft and juicy. With the sweetness of the tomatoes it made a very special sauce.

You can also use this pesto on a bruschetta as a starter.

ingredients.item: very ripe tomatoes, skin and seeds removed, roughly chopped
dried salted ricotta or parmigiano, freshly grated

method: Put all the pesto ingredients into a mortar or food processor and blend to a coarse paste. Taste and adjust the seasoning to your liking.

Boil the pasta in plenty of salted water. As soon as the pasta is cooked al dente, drain it and tip it into a warm serving dish. Pour on the pesto and toss well. Take to the table straight away and serve with the ricotta or parmigiano on the side.
